### Post by CarlWalters on 2012-08-19
I've just started receiving e-mails from anacron like this 
```

/etc/cron.daily/standard:

Items were found in lost+found directories. This is probably the result
of a crash or bad shutdown, or possibly of a disk problem. You should
examine them to see if they contain important information, and either
retrieve them from lost+found or delete them.

The following items were found:

/lost+found:
        #8126706
/etc/cron.daily/update-notifier-common:
flashplugin-installer: downloading [http://archive.canonical.com/pool/partner/a/adobe-flashplugin/adobe-flashplugin_11.2.202.238.orig.tar.gz](http://archive.canonical.com/pool/partner/a/adobe-flashplugin/adobe-flashplugin_11.2.202.238.orig.tar.gz)
Installing from local file /tmp/tmptj8Rdu.gz
Flash Plugin installed.

```

Do I need to worry about these?  I tried looking at the /lost+found:#8126706 file but it doesn't make much sense :)

### Post by Rexilion on 2012-08-19
Doesn't look like important information to me. Generally, if you did not update during the unclean shutdown then you mostly lose temporary files. Well, except for real documents you are editing of course.

I was not able to trace the origin of that file. Move it out of lost+found and the message goes away. You can keep it for a while and throw it away later.

---
